text: fat is 51.5g. polyunsaturated fat is one of the better parts of the profile. for seeds, the fat profile is one of the biggest reasons they earn their place.
carbs is 20g. starch is doing most of the heavy lifting here. for a seed, the best ones punch way above their size.
protein is 20.8g. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein is a bonus, but not enough by itself.
vitamin e at 175% DV stand out most. that is major vitamin support.
magnesium at 104% DV, zinc at 45% DV, iron at 29% DV stand out most. that gives this food real mineral support.

pros first. tiny food with serious nutrient density because seeds often overperform for their size. easy to sprinkle into meals because that practical versatility matters. usually brings decent fat support because that is one of the category strengths.

on the weaker side. calorie density still stacks up fast because tiny foods can still overshoot fast. real serving sizes are often small because some strong paper numbers are hard to cash out in practice. texture or prep can be a barrier because not everyone uses seeds consistently.
B tier. Best as a support food that boosts meals instead of carrying them alone.
